message.content:
I need to understand the eligibility approval flow in the handset service.

1. Read `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/models/models.go` — show me the full `EligibilityOutcome` struct.

2. Read `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/service/service.go` — find the call to `UpdateContractEligibilityApproved` and show me the surrounding code (what `EligibilityOutcome` is constructed with and what data is available at that point).

3. Read `/Users/dumitruvulpe/uw/telecom-mobile/services/handset/internal/storage/storage.go` — show me the full `UpdateContractEligibilityApproved` function body.

4. Also check: does `EligibilityOutcome` include `ContractMonths`? If not, is `ContractMonths` available at the call site in service.go (e.g., from the contract being retrieved)?

Report all four points clearly.

attachment.content:
- frontend-design:frontend-design: Create distinctive, production-grade frontend interfaces with high design quality. Use this skill when the user asks to build web components, pages, or applications. Generates creative, polished code that avoids generic AI aesthetics.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll read these files in parallel to understand the eligibility approval flow.

content.content:
1	package models
2	
3	import (
4		"time"
5	
6		"github.com/google/uuid"
7	)
8	
9	type ContractStatus string
10	
11	const (
12		ContractStatusPendingEligibility  ContractStatus = "PENDING_ELIGIBILITY"
13		ContractStatusEligibilityApproved ContractStatus = "ELIGIBILITY_APPROVED"
14		ContractStatusApproved            ContractStatus = "APPROVED"
15		ContractStatusDeclined            ContractStatus = "DECLINED"
16		ContractStatusHandsetOrdered      ContractStatus = "HANDSET_ORDERED"
17		ContractStatusExpired             ContractStatus = "EXPIRED"
18	)
19	
20	type Handset struct {
21		ID                uuid.UUID           `json:"id" bson:"id"`
22		BillID            string              `json:"billID" bson:"billID"`
23		Active            bool                `json:"active" bson:"active"`
24		QuantityInStock   int                 `json:"quantityInStock" bson:"quantityInStock"`
25		CurrentProperties HandsetProperties   `json:"currentProperties" bson:"currentProperties"`
26		Versions          []HandsetProperties `json:"versions" bson:"versions"`
27	}
28	
29	type HandsetProperties struct {
30		Version            string    `bson:"version" json:"version"`
31		Name               string    `bson:"name" json:"name"`
32		RentalPricePence   int       `bson:"rentalPricePence" json:"rentalPricePence"`
33		RRPPence           int       `bson:"rrpPence" json:"rrpPence"`
34		ContractTermMonths int       `bson:"contractTermMonths" json:"contractTermMonths"`
35		Manufacturer       string    `bson:"manufacturer" json:"manufacturer"`
36		Model              string    `bson:"model" json:"model"`
37		Colours            string    `bson:"colours" json:"colours"`
38		Capacity           string    `bson:"capacity" json:"capacity"`
39		ScreenSize         string    `bson:"screenSize" json:"screenSize"`
40		Battery            string    `bson:"battery" json:"battery"`
41		SDCapacity         string    `bson:"sdCapacity" json:"sdCapacity"`
42		WiFiStandard       string    `bson:"wiFiStandard" json:"wiFiStandard"`
43		ESim               bool      `bson:"eSim" json:"eSim"`
44		DualSim            bool      `bson:"dualSim" json:"dualSim"`
45		SaleStatus         string    `bson:"saleStatus" json:"saleStatus"`
46		IsNew              bool      `bson:"isNew" json:"isNew"`
47		UWLaunchDate       time.Time `bson:"uwLaunchDate" json:"uwLaunchDate"`
48		PriceChange        bool      `bson:"priceChange" json:"priceChange"`
49		EndOfLife          bool      `bson:"endOfLife" json:"endOfLife"`
50		SpecificationsLink string    `bson:"specificationsLink" json:"specificationsLink"`
51		BillID             string    `bson:"billID" json:"billID"`
52	}
53	
54	type Contract struct {
55		ID                 uuid.UUID  `bson:"id"`
56		ServiceID          string     `bson:"serviceId"`
57		CustomerID         string     `bson:"customerId"`
58		HandsetID          string     `bson:"handsetId"`
59		HandsetName        string     `bson:"handsetName"`
60		HandsetVersion     string     `bson:"handsetVersion"`
61		ContractPrice      string     `bson:"contractPrice"`
62		RetailPrice        string     `bson:"retailPrice"`
63		ContractMonths     string     `bson:"contractMonths"`
64		RequestID          string     `bson:"requestId"`
65		LoanReference      string     `bson:"loanReference,omitempty"`
66		BankAccountNumber  string     `bson:"bankAccountNumber,omitempty"`
67		BankTitle          string     `bson:"bankTitle,omitempty"`
68		Decision           string     `bson:"decision,omitempty"`
69		AgreementID        string     `bson:"agreementId,omitempty"`
70		DocusignEnvelopeID string     `bson:"docusignEnvelopeId,omitempty"`
71		SignedAt           *time.Time `bson:"signedAt,omitempty"`
72		// This field is calculated when handset is ordered
73		ExpiresAt      *time.Time     `bson:"expiresAt,omitempty"`
74		CompletedAt    *time.Time     `bson:"completedAt,omitempty"`
75		FailureReason  string         `bson:"failureReason,omitempty"`
76		FailureStage   string         `bson:"failureStage,omitempty"`
77		FailureMessage string         `bson:"failureMessage,omitempty"`
78		FailedAt       *time.Time     `bson:"failedAt,omitempty"`
79		Status         ContractStatus `bson:"status"`
80		CreatedAt      time.Time      `bson:"createdAt"`
81		UpdatedAt      time.Time      `bson:"updatedAt"`
82	}
83	
84	type EligibilityOutcome struct {
85		Decision           string
86		AgreementID        string
87		DocusignEnvelopeID string
88		SignedAt           *time.Time
89		CompletedAt        *time.Time
90	}
91	
92	type EligibilityFailureOutcome struct {
93		FailureReason  string
94		FailureStage   string
95		FailureMessage string
96		FailedAt       *time.Time
97	}
98
